title: Single-cell suspensions from primary human esophagus tissue
author: Annette Hayden
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.t9ver66

steps.title: Transfer the minced tissue to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the minced tissue to the Collagenase/DNAse solution in a 50ml Falcon.

steps.title: Shake the suspension at 110-150 rpm...
steps.instruction: Shake the suspension at 110-150 rpm for 60 mins at 37°C (with the tube on its side to increase agitation of the tissue).

steps.title: After 15, 30, and 60 mins...
steps.instruction: After 15, 30, and 60 mins – pipette up and down with descending sized pipettes (25, 10 and 5 ml).

steps.title: Strain with a 70 um cell strainer
steps.instruction: Strain with a 70 um cell strainer.

steps.title: Add 10 ml DMEM (empty) to...
steps.instruction: Add 10 ml DMEM (empty) to sieve and push through with syringe plunger.

steps.title: Keep on ice from this point
steps.instruction: Keep on ice from this point.

steps.title: Spin 1500 rpm for 5mins and remove media
steps.instruction: Spin 1500 rpm for 5mins and remove media.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in RBC lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in RBC lysis buffer and incubate @ 4°C for 10mins.

steps.title: Add 10 ml DMEM (empty) and...
steps.instruction: Add 10 ml DMEM (empty) and pipette up and down thoroughly.

steps.title: Pass through 40 um cell strainer...
steps.instruction: Pass through 40 um cell strainer – pushing through with plunger.

steps.title: Spin at 1500 rpm for 5 mins and remove media
steps.instruction: Spin at 1500 rpm for 5 mins and remove media.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 1 ml of cell suspension buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 1 ml of cell suspension buffer.

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.5ml tube, add 10 ul cell solution with 10 ul trypan blue.

steps.title: Add resultant 20 ul mix onto...
steps.instruction: Add resultant 20 ul mix onto disposable haemocytometer (C-Chip, FR type).

steps.title: Count the cells and record cell viability
steps.instruction: Count the cells and record cell viability
